programs.programName: NC Lifespan Respite Program / Family Caregiver Support

verified.notes: NC Lifespan Respite Program serves caregivers of individuals of ANY age (not age-restricted to seniors). No age, income, or asset eligibility criteria are specified in official sources. Program is application-based with funding limitations. Phone number is for Caregiver Program Coordinator Tammy Nelson at High Country Area Agency on Aging (ext. 139). Alternative contact: lifespan@hccog.org. Program provides up to $750/calendar year in respite care vouchers. Applicants must be referred by a local professional organization and use funds within 90 days.

verified.notes: No official .gov source found for 2026 LTC Medicaid details; used current nonprofit citing official limits. Nursing home Medicaid has no age test for seniors but requires nursing facility level of care. Income has no fixed cap (must be < nursing home rate). Assets for couple reflect $2000 applicant + CSRA; both-applying may differ per source.

programs.programName: Project C.A.R.E. (Caregiver Alternatives to Running on Empty)

verified.notes: Project C.A.R.E. is a dementia-specific caregiver support program with no apparent age, income, or asset eligibility requirements based on available official sources. The program serves family caregivers of individuals with Alzheimer's disease and related dementias across all 100 North Carolina counties. Phone number obtained from state project director contact information. No financial eligibility criteria found in official NC DHHS documentation or RFA materials.
